_note: Standard DORA four-keys bands (Google DORA / Accelerate, State of DevOps). Bands are the public industry reference; compute-dora.mjs classifies the framework's own wave-delivery proxy against them. This is a published standard, not fabricated data.

metrics.name: Deployment Frequency
metrics.rationale: How often the team ships to mainline. Framework proxy (D2): unique waves delivered via conventional-commit subjects (feat(scope): wave-NN).

bands.high: Between once per day and once per week (>= 1/week)
bands.medium: Between once per week and once per month

metrics.name: Lead Time for Changes
metrics.rationale: Time from commit to it being live on mainline. Framework proxy: time from spec approval to wave-complete tag.

bands.high: Between one day and one week
bands.medium: Between one week and one month

metrics.name: Change Failure Rate
metrics.rationale: Share of deployments causing a failure requiring remediation (revert, hotfix, rollback). Framework proxy: waves with a follow-up fix(...) revert commit.

metrics.name: Failed Deployment Recovery Time
metrics.rationale: Time to restore service after a failed change. Framework proxy: time from a halt-event to the matching andon-resume.

ai_calibration_note: These are human-team DORA bands applied to an agent-driven pipeline as a proxy. Deployment = wave delivery to mainline, not a production release (the framework orchestrates Claude Code; it does not deploy a service). Treat absolute bands as directional until a real deploy target is wired.
